standard_description: Recognize situations in which one quantity changes at a constant rate per unit interval relative to another.
subject: Math

problems.text: You deposit $8,000 into an account that pays 4.5% interest, compounded quarterly. How long will it take for you to have $12,000?

Potassium-40 has a half-life of
$${1.28\times 10^9}$$
years, which means that after that many years, half of a sample has decayed into a different isotope of carbon. If a sample began with
$${115}$$
grams of potassium-40
$${30,000,000}$$
years ago, how much is left today? How long will it be until there are only
$${50}$$
grams remaining?

Problem 1
The graph of a function of the form
$${f(x)=ab^x}$$
is shown below. Find the values of
$$a$$
and
$$b$$
.
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
A fisherman illegally introduces some fish into a lake, and they quickly propagate. The growth of the population of this new species (within a period of a few years) is modeled by
$${P(x)=5b^x}$$
, where
$$x$$
is the time in weeks following the introduction and
$$b$$
is a positive unknown base.
Exactly how many fish did the fisherman release into the lake?
Find
$$b$$
is you know the lake contains
$${33}$$
fish after eight weeks. Show step-by-step work.
Instead, now suppose that
$${P(x)=5b^x}$$
and
$$b=2$$
What is the weekly percent growth rate in this case? What does this mean in every-day language?
Problem 3
Do the following equations represent growth or decay?
$${y=2(3)^{x\over5}}$$
$${y=2\left ( 1\over3 \right )^x}$$
$${y=2(3)^{-x}}$$
$${y={1\over2}(3)^x}$$

Task
​Raphael deposits \$10,000 in a bank account which earns 5% interest, compounded annually. If he makes no other deposits or withdrawals, how long will it take for Raphael to have \$50,000 in his bank account?
The Alpe d'Huez is a famous climb in the Tour de France. The road is about 14 kilometers long. It starts at an elevation of about 740 meters and the elevation gain is about 8% of the distance traveled on the road. What is the approximate elevation after 10 kilometers of the climb? What about at the top?
A sample of Radon 222 has a mass of 100 grams. It is radioactive with a half-life of 3.8 days (that is, after every 3.8 days, half of the Radon 222 decays, becoming Polonium 218). Approximately when will 1 gram of the Radon sample remain?

metadata.title: Solving Problems with Linear and Exponential Models

problems.text:
Problem 1
Below is a visual representation of a pattern of growth of a particular kind of bacterium.
###IMAGE0###
How many bacteria are there after 20 minutes?
Problem 2
Have students complete Dan Meyer's Three-Act Task
Incredible Shrinking Dollar
.
Problem 3
Show that the following two functions are equivalent:
$${f(n)=3(2)^{x-1}}$$
and
$${f(n)=1.5(2)^x}$$

Task
The below table provides some U.S. Population data from 1982 to 1988:
###TABLE0###
Notice: The change in population from 1982 to 1983 is 2,128,000, which is recorded in thousands in the first row of the 3rd column. The other changes are computed similarly. All population numbers in the table are recorded in thousands.
Source: http://www.census.gov/popest/archives/1990s/popclockest.txt
If we were to model the relationship between the U.S. population and the year, would a linear
function be appropriate? Explain why or why not.
Mike decides to use a linear function to model the relationship.  He chooses 2,139, the average of the values in the 3rd column, for the slope. What meaning does this value have in the context of this model?
Use Mike's model to predict the U.S. population in 1992.

Problem 1
Suppose that water is entering a cylindrical water tank so that the initial height of the water is
$$3$$
cm and the height of the water doubles every
$$30$$
seconds. Write an equation of the height of the water at time
$$t$$
seconds.
Problem 2
You put $500 in a savings account that advertises 5% interest.
Bank A pays interest once a year, and the interest model is
$${B=500(1.05)^t}$$
, where
$$B$$
is the balance in the account after
$$t$$
years.
Bank B pays interest monthly, and the interest model is
$$B=500(1+{.05\over12})^{12t}$$
, where
$$B$$
is the balance in the account after
$$t$$
years.
Which bank will yield more money after 10 years?

According to Wikipedia, the International Basketball Federation (FIBA) requires that a basketball bounce to a height of
$${1300}$$
mm when dropped from a height of
$${1800}$$
mm.
Suppose you drop a basketball and the ratio of each rebound height to the previous rebound height is
$${1300}:{1800}$$
. Let
$$h$$
be the function that assigns to
$$n$$
the rebound height of the ball (in mm) on the
$$n^{th}$$
bounce. Complete the chart below, rounding to the nearest mm.
###TABLE0###
Write an expression for
$$h(n)$$
.

Task
The below table provides some U.S. Population data from 1982 to 1988:
###TABLE0###
Notice: The change in population from 1982 to 1983 is 2,128,000, which is recorded in thousands in the first row of the 3rd column. The other changes are computed similarly. All population numbers in the table are recorded in thousands.
Source: http://www.census.gov/popest/archives/1990s/popclockest.txt
If we were to model the relationship between the U.S. population and the year, would a linear
function be appropriate? Explain why or why not.
Mike decides to use a linear function to model the relationship.  He chooses 2,139, the average of the values in the 3rd column, for the slope. What meaning does this value have in the context of this model?
Use Mike's model to predict the U.S. population in 1992.

Task
The figure below shows the graphs of the functions represented by $f(x)=4x-a$ and $g(x)=2x+b$ for some constants $a$ and $b$.  They intersect at the point $(p,q)$.
###IMAGE0###
Label the graph of $f$. Do the same for the graph of $g$.
What do $a$ and $b$ represent in the graphs above?
Imagine you place the tip of your pencil at point $(p,q)$ and trace the graph of $f$ out to the point with $x$-coordinate $p+2$.  Imagine I do the same on the graph of $g$.  How much greater would the $y$-coordinate of your ending point be than mine?

Problem 1
Lincoln deposits money in a Certificate of Deposit account. The balance (in dollars) in his account t years after making the deposit is given by
$${{{{L(t)}}}=500(1.05)^t}$$
for
$${{t\geq0}}$$
. Explain, in terms of the structure of the expression for
$${{{L(t)}}}$$
, why Lincoln's balance can never be
$${499}$$
.
Helen deposits money in a similar Certificate of Deposit account. The balance in her account is described, with units as in Lincoln's deposit,
$${{{H(t)}}=600(1.04)^t}$$
for
$${{t\geq0}}$$
. Use the structure of the expressions for
$${{{L(t)}}}$$
and
$${{H(t)}}$$
to describe how the two balances compare over time.
By what percent does the value of
$${{{L(t)}}}$$
grow each year? What about
$${{H(t)}}$$
? Explain.
During which year does Lincoln's balance to "catch up" with Helen's? Show your work.
Problem 2
A cup of hot coffee will, over time, cool down to room temperature. The principle of physics governing the process is Newton's Law of Cooling. Experiments with a covered cup of coffee show that the temperature (in degrees Fahrenheit) of the coffee can be modelled by the following equation
$${f(t)=110e^{−0.08t}+75}$$
Here the time
$$t$$
is measured in minutes after the coffee was poured into the cup.
Explain, using the structure of the expression
$$ 110e^{−0.08t}+75$$
, why the coffee temperature decreases as time elapses.
What is the temperature of the coffee at the beginning of the experiment?
After how many minutes is the coffee 140 degrees? After how many minutes is the coffee 100 degrees?

Task
For each or the scenarios below, decide whether the situation can be modeled by a linear function, an exponential function, or neither. For those with a linear or exponential model, create a function which accurately describes the situation.
From 1910 until 2010 the growth rate of the United States has been steady at about 1.5% per year. The population in 1910 was about 92,000,000.
The circumference of a circle as a function of the radius.
According to an old legend, an Indian King played a game of chess with a traveling sage on a beautiful, hand-made chessboard. The sage requested, as reward for winning the game, one grain of rice for the first square, two grains for the second, four grains for the third, and so on for the whole chess board. How many grains of rice would the sage win for the $n^{\rm th}$ square?
The volume of a cube as a function of its side length.

Task
The following tables show the values of linear, quadratic, and exponential functions at various values of $x$.  Indicate which function type corresponds to each table. Justify your choice.
###TABLE0###

Task
The data in the table below was taken from Wikipedia.
###TABLE0###
Based on the data in the above table, would a linear function be
appropriate to model the
relationship between the
world population and the year?  Explain how you know.
Using only the data from 1960 onward in the above table,
would a linear function be appropriate to approximate
the
relationship between the
world population and the year?  Explain how you know.
Based on your work in parts (a) and (b), would a linear function
be appropriate to  predict the world
population in 2200?  Explain.

metadata.title: In The Billions and Linear Modeling

problems.text:
A man invests $1,000 in an account with a 5% annual percentage rate.
Write equations that model the balance in the account,
$$B$$
, if the interest is compounded at the following rates in
$$t$$
years.
Semi-annually
Quarterly
Monthly
Daily
Which rate will make the most in interest over a given period of time?

Problem 1
A population of a city grows at 5% per year. The population in 1990 was 400,000. In what year do you predict the population will reach one million?
Problem 2
A bacteria doubles in size every six hours. Four hours from now, the culture has 8,000 bacteria. How many bacteria were there to start?
Problem 3
Algal blooms routinely threaten the health of the Chesapeake Bay. Phosphate compounds supply a rich source of nutrients for the algae,
Prorocentrum minimum
, responsible for particularly harmful spring blooms known as mahogany tides. These compounds are found in fertilizers used by farmers and find their way into the Bay with run-offs resulting from rainstorms. Favorable conditions result in rapid algae growth ranging anywhere from 0.144 to 2.885 cell divisions per day. Algae concentrations are measured and reported in terms of cells per milliliter (cells/ml). Concentrations in excess of 3,000 cells/ml constitute a bloom.
Suppose that heavy spring rains followed by sunny days create conditions that support 1 cell division per day and that prior to the rains
Prorocentrum minimum
concentrations measured just 10 cells/ml. Write an equation for a function that models the relationship between the algae concentration and the number of days since the algae began to divide at the rate of 1 cell division per day.
Assuming this rate of cell divison is sustained for 10 days, present the resulting algae concentrations over that period in a table. Did these conditions result in a bloom?
If conditions support 2 cell divisions per day, when will these conditions result in a bloom?
Concentrations in excess of 200,000 cells/ml have been reported in the Bay. Assuming the same conditions as in (c), when will concentrations exceed 200,000 cells/ml?

khan_exercises.title: Interpret change in exponential models
khan_exercises.description: Given a function that models an exponential situation, find the factor by which the quantity changes per unit time interval.
topic_path: Topic D: Lessons 25-28: Modeling with exponential functions

items.question_content:
On the first day of spring, an entire field of flowering trees blossoms. The population of locusts consuming these flowers  rapidly increases as the trees blossom.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in days, since the beginning of spring, and the total number of locusts, $L(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$L(t)=750\cdot \left(1.85\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the daily rate of change of the locust population.**

Every day, the locust population  [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in population?

The exponential function modeling the population of locusts is of the form $L(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of locusts (on the first day of spring) and **$B$ determines the daily change** in the population. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in population

The number of locusts is *multiplied by $\it{1.85}$ every day*. Since $1.85>1$,  the population **grows** by a factor of $1.85$ every day.

### Summary

Every day, the locust population grows by a factor of $1.85$.

items.question_content:
Nora uploaded a funny video on her website, which rapidly gains views over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in days, since Nora uploaded the video, and the total number of views, $V(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$V(t)=100\cdot \left(1.53\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the daily rate of change in the views of the video.**

Every day, the number of views  [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in the number of views?

The exponential function modeling the number of views is of the form $V(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of views (when the video was uploaded) and **$B$ determines the daily change** in the number of views. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in number of views

The number of views is *multiplied by $\it{1.53}$ every day*. Since $1.53>1$,  the number of views **grows** by a factor of $1.53$ every day.

### Summary

Every day, the number of views grows by a factor of $1.53$.

items.question_content:
Takumi started studying how the number of branches on his tree changes over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in years, since Takumi started studying his tree, and the total number of its branches, $N(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$N(t)=63\cdot 2\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the yearly rate of change in the number of branches.**

Every year, the number of branches [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in the number of branches?

The exponential function modeling the number of branches is of the form $N(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of branches (when Takumi began studying his tree) and **$B$ determines the yearly change** in the number of branches. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in number of branches

The number of branches is *multiplied by $\it{2}$ every year*. Since $2>1$,  the number of branches **grows** by a factor of $2$ every year.

### Summary

Every year, the number of branches grows by a factor of $2$.

items.question_content:
Pavlo invested some money in a bank account.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in months, since Pavlo invested the money, and the total amount of money in the account, $M(t)$, in dollars, is modeled by the following function:

###$M(t)=1000\cdot (1.01)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the monthly rate of change in the amount of money in the account.**

Every month, the amount of money in the account [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in the amount of money?

The exponential function modeling the amount of money in the account is of the form $M(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial amount of money (when Pavlo invested the money) and **$B$ determines the monthly change** in the amount of money. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in amount of money

The amount of money is *multiplied by $\it{1.01}$ every month*. Since $1.01>1$,  the amount of money in the account **grows** by a factor of $1.01$ every month.

### Summary

Every month, the amount of money in the account grows by a factor of $1.01$.

items.question_content:
Elon sent a chain letter to his friends, asking them to forward the letter to more friends.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in days, since Elon sent the email, and the total number of people who receive the email, $P(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$P(t)=4\cdot 3\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the daily rate of change in the number of people who receive the email.**

Every day, the number of people who receive the email [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in the number of people who receive the email?

The exponential function modeling the number of people who receive the email is of the form $P(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of people who receive the email (when Elon sent it) and **$B$ determines the daily rate of change** in the number of people who receive the email. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in number of people who receive the email

The number of people who receive the email is *multiplied by $\it{3}$ every day*. Since $3>1$,  the number of people who received the email **grows** by a factor of $3$ every day.

### Summary

Every day, the number of people who receive the email grows by a factor of $3$.

items.question_content:
Vera is an ecologist who studies the change in the bear population of Siberia over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in years, since Vera began studying the population, and the total number of bears, $N(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$N(t)=2187\cdot \left(\dfrac{2}{3}\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the yearly rate of change of the bear population.**

Every year, the bear population  [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in population?

The exponential function modeling the population of bears is of the form $N(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of bears (when Vera began studying them) and **$B$ determines the yearly change** in the population. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in population

The number of bears in Siberia is *multiplied by $\it{\dfrac{2}{3}}$ every year*. Since $\dfrac 23<1$,  the population **shrinks** by a factor of $\dfrac23$ every year.

### Summary

Every year, the bear population shrinks by a factor of $\dfrac 23$.

items.question_content:
Clemence began studying a sample of the chemical element einsteinium-$253$, which  naturally loses its mass over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in days, since Clemence started studying the sample, and the total mass remaining in the sample, $M(t)$, in milligrams, is modeled by the following function:

###$M(t)=200\cdot (0.96)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the daily rate of change in the mass of the sample.**

Every day, the mass of the sample [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in mass?

The exponential function modeling the mass of the sample is of the form $M(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial mass of the sample (when Clemence began studying it) and **$B$ determines the daily change** in the mass of the sample. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in mass

The mass of the sample is *multiplied by $\it{0.96}$ every day*. Since $0.96<1$,  the mass of the sample **shrinks** by a factor of $0.96$ every day.

### Summary

Every day, the mass of the sample shrinks by a factor of $0.96$.

items.question_content:
After the last ice age began, the number of plant species in South America changed rapidly.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in years, since the ice age began, and the total number of plant species, $P(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$P(t)=30{,}000{,}000\cdot \left(\dfrac{8}{9}\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the yearly rate of change of the number of plant species.**

Every year, the number of plant species  [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in the number of plant species?

The exponential function modeling the number of plant species is of the form $P(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of plant species (when ice age began) and **$B$ determines the yearly change** in the number of plant species. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in the number of plant species

The number of plant species is *multiplied by $\it{\dfrac{8}{9}}$ every year*. Since $\dfrac 89<1$,  the number of plant species **shrinks** by a factor of $\dfrac89$ every year.

### Summary

Every year, the number of plant species shrinks by a factor of $\dfrac 89$.

items.question_content:
After a certain medicine is ingested, its concentration in the bloodstream changes over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in minutes, since the medicine was ingested, and its concentration in the bloodstream, $C(t)$, in $\text{mg/L}$ is modeled by the following function:

###$C(t)=95\cdot (0.87)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the rate of change in the concentration of the medicine.**

Every minute, the concentration of the medicine [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in medicine concentration?

The exponential function modeling the concentration of the medicine is of the form $C(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial concentration of the medicine (when  it is ingested) and **$B$ determines the rate of change** in the concentration of medicine every minute. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in medicine concentration

The concentration of the medicine is *multiplied by $\it{0.87}$ every minute*. Since $0.87<1$,  the concentration of the medicine **shrinks** by a factor of $0.87$ every minute.

### Summary

Every minute, the concentration of the medicine shrinks by a factor of $0.87$.

items.question_content:
When Zakir threw a beach ball to his friend, its horizontal velocity changed as it traveled through the air.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in seconds, since Zakir threw the ball, and its horizontal velocity, $V(t)$, in $\text{cm/s}$ is modeled by the following function:

###$V(t)=4\cdot \left(\dfrac{5}{8}\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the rate of change of the horizontal velocity of the ball.**

Every second, the horizontal velocity of the ball  [[☃ dropdown 2]] by a factor of [[☃ numeric-input 1]].
hints:
###What determines the change in the horizontal velocity?

The exponential function modeling the horizontal velocity of the ball is of the form $V(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial horizontal velocity of the ball (when Zakir threw it) and **$B$ determines the rate of change** in the horizontal velocity every second. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in the horizontal velocity

The horizontal velocity of the ball is *multiplied by $\it{\dfrac{5}{8}}$ every second*. Since $\dfrac 58<1$,  the horizontal velocity  **shrinks** by a factor of $\dfrac58$ every second.

### Summary

Every second, the horizontal velocity of the ball shrinks by a factor of $\dfrac 58$.

items.question_content:
On the first day of spring, an entire field of flowering trees blossoms. The population of locusts consuming these flowers  rapidly increases as the trees blossom.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in days, since the beginning of spring, and the total number of locusts, $L(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$L(t)=1200\cdot \left(1.65\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the daily percent change of the locust population.**

Every day, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of locusts are [[☃ dropdown 1]] the locust population.
hints:
###What determines the change in population?

The exponential function modeling the population of locusts is of the form $L(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of locusts (on the first day of spring) and **$B$ determines the daily change** in the population. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in population

The number of locusts is multiplied by $1.\blueD{65}$ daily. This means that $\blueD{65}\%$ of locusts are *added to* the population every day.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every day, $65 \%$ of locusts are added to the locust population.

items.question_content:
Nina uploaded a funny video on her website, which rapidly gains views over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in days, since Nina uploaded the video, and the total number of views, $V(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$V(t)=500\cdot \left(1.8\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the daily percent change in the views of the video.**

Every day, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of views are [[☃ dropdown 3]] the total number of views of the video.
hints:
###What determines the change in the number of views?

The exponential function modeling the number of views is of the form $V(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of views (when the video was uploaded) and **$B$ determines the daily change** in the number of views. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in number of views

The number of views is *multiplied by $1.\blueD{8}$ every day*. This means that $\blueD{80}\%$ of views are are *added to* the total number of views every day.   [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every day, $80 \%$ of views are added to the total number of views.

items.question_content:
Akiba started studying how the number of branches on his tree changes over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in years, since Akiba started studying his tree, and the total number of its branches, $N(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$N(t)=42\cdot (1.75)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the yearly percent change in the number of branches.**

Every year, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of branches are [[☃ dropdown 3]] the total number of branches.
hints:
###What determines the change in the number of branches?

The exponential function modeling the number of branches is of the form $N(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of branches (when Akiba began studying his tree) and **$B$ determines the yearly change** in the number of branches. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in number of branches

The number of branches is *multiplied by $1.\blueD{75}$ every year*. This means that $\blueD{75}\%$ of branches are *added to* the total number of branches every year.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every year, $75 \%$ of branches are added to the total number of branches.

items.question_content:
Qiaochu invested some money in a bank account.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in months, since Qiaochu invested the money, and the total amount of money in the account, $M(t)$, in dollars, is modeled by the following function:

###$M(t)=3200\cdot (1.02)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the monthly percent change in the amount of money in the account.**

Every month, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of money is [[☃ dropdown 3]] the total amount of money in the account.
hints:
###What determines the change in the amount of money?


The exponential function modeling the amount of money in the account is of the form $M(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial amount of money (when Qiaochu invested the money) and **$B$ determines the monthly change** in the amount of money. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in amount of money

The amount of money is *multiplied by $1.\blueD{02}$ every month*. This means that $\blueD{2}\%$ of money is *added to* the total  amount of money in the account every month. [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every month, $2 \%$ of money is added to the total amount of money in the account.

items.question_content:
Markus sent a chain letter to his friends, asking them to forward the letter to more friends.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in days, since Markus sent the email, and the total number of people who receive the email, $P(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$P(t)=6\cdot (1.43)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the daily percent change in the number of people who receive the email.**

Every day, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of people are [[☃ dropdown 3]] the total number of people who receive the email.
hints:
###What determines the change in the number of people who receive the email?

The exponential function modeling the number of people who receive the email is of the form $P(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of people who receive the email (when Markus sent it) and **$B$ determines the daily rate of change** in the number of people who receive the email. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in number of people who receive the email

The number of people who receive the email is *multiplied by $1.\blueD{43}$ every day*. This means that $\blueD{43}\%$ of people are *added to* the total number of people who receive the email every day.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every day, $43 \%$ of people are added to the total number of people who receive the email.

items.question_content:
Alina is an ecologist who studies the change in the bear population of Siberia over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in years, since Alina began studying the population, and the total number of bears, $N(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$N(t)=2187\cdot \left(0.67\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the yearly percent change of the bear population.**

Every year, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of bears are [[☃ dropdown 3]] the bear population in Siberia.
hints:
###What determines the change in population?

The exponential function modeling the population of bears is of the form $N(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of bears (when Alina began studying them) and **$B$ determines the yearly change** in the population. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in population

The number of bears in Siberia is *multiplied by $0.\blueD{67}$ every year*. This means that $\blueD{33}\%$ of bears are *subtracted from*  the bear population every year.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every year, $33 \%$ of bears are subtracted from the total bear population in Siberia.

items.question_content:
Melanie began studying a sample of the chemical element einsteinium-$253$, which  naturally loses its mass over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in days, since Melanie started studying the sample, and the total mass remaining in the sample, $M(t)$, in micrograms, is modeled by the following function:

###$M(t)=169\cdot (0.96)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the daily percent change in the mass of the sample.**

Every day, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of mass is [[☃ dropdown 3]] the total mass of the sample.
hints:
###What determines the change in mass?

The exponential function modeling the mass of the sample is of the form $M(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial mass of the sample (when Melanie began studying it) and **$B$ determines the daily change** in the mass of the sample. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in mass

The mass of the sample is *multiplied by $0.\blueD{96}$  every day*. This means that $\blueD{4}\%$ of mass is *subtracted from*  the mass of the sample every day.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every day, $4 \%$ of mass is subtracted from the total mass of the sample.

items.question_content:
After the last ice age began, the number of animal species in Australia changed rapidly.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in years, since the ice age began, and the total number of animal species, $S(t)$, is modeled by the following function:

###$S(t)=4{,}200{,}000\cdot \left(0.72\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the yearly percent change of the number of animal species.**

Every year, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of species are [[☃ dropdown 3]] the total number of animal species.
hints:
###What determines the change in the number of animal species?

The exponential function modeling the number of animal species is of the form $S(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial number of animal species (when ice age began) and **$B$ determines the yearly change** in the number of animal species. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in the number of animal species

The number of animal species is *multiplied by $0.\blueD{72}$ every year*. This means that $\blueD{28}\%$ of species are *subtracted from*  the total number of animal species every year. [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every year, $28 \%$ of species are subtracted from the total number of animal species in Australia.

items.question_content:
After a certain medicine is ingested, its concentration in the bloodstream changes over time.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in minutes, since the medicine was ingested, and its concentration in the bloodstream, $C(t)$, in $\text{mg/L}$, is modeled by the following function:

###$C(t)=78\cdot (0.62)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the percent change in the concentration of the medicine.**

Every minute, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of concentration is [[☃ dropdown 3]] the total concentration of the medicine in the bloodstream.
hints:
###What determines the change in medicine concentration?

The exponential function modeling the concentration of the medicine is of the form $C(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial concentration of the medicine (when  it is ingested) and **$B$ determines the rate of change** in the concentration of medicine every minute. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in medicine concentration

The concentration of the medicine is *multiplied by $0.\blueD{62}$ every minute*. This means that $\blueD{38}\%$ of concentration is *subtracted from*   the total concentration of the medicine every minute. [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every minute, $38 \%$ of concentration is subtracted from the total concentration of the medicine in the bloodstream.

items.question_content:
When Mustafa threw a beach ball to his friend, its horizontal velocity changed as it traveled through the air.

The relationship between the elapsed time, $t$, in seconds, since Mustafa threw the ball, and its horizontal velocity, $V(t)$, in  $\text{cm/s}$, is modeled by the following function:

###$V(t)=4\cdot \left(0.81\right)\^{\Large t}$

**Complete the following sentence about the percent change of the horizontal velocity of the ball.**

Every second, [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\%$ of horizontal velocity is [[☃ dropdown 3]] the total horizontal velocity of the ball.
hints:
###What determines the change in the horizontal velocity?

The exponential function modeling the horizontal velocity of the ball is of the form $V(t) = A \cdot B\^{\Large t}$. Therefore, $A$ determines the initial horizontal velocity of the ball (when Mustafa threw it) and **$B$ determines the rate of change** in the horizontal velocity every second. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How to find the change in the horizontal velocity

The horizontal velocity of the ball is *multiplied by $0.\blueD{81}$ every second*. This means that $\blueD{19}\%$ of horizontal velocity is *subtracted from* the total horizontal velocity every second.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

### Summary

Every second, $19 \%$ of horizontal velocity is subtracted from the total horizontal velocity of the ball.
